Task ID: FIX-SECURITY-AUTH-001
Agent: senior-security-engineer
Task: Cross-tenant auth bypass fix on 19 routes + TOTP verification + payments/config tenant scope

Work Log:

**Fix 1 ŌĆö /api/trafficker POST `withdraw` action (P0, financial theft):**
- Imported `verifyTOTP` from `@/lib/totp` and `Session` type from `next-auth`.
- Added `authorizeTraffickerAccess(session, traffickerEmail)` helper that returns a 403 NextResponse when the caller is neither the trafficker (case-insensitive email match) nor an admin/finance operator; null otherwise.
- `withdraw()` now takes `session: Session | null` (passed in from POST dispatcher) and:
  1. Fetches the trafficker via `traffickerService.getTraffickerById(traffickerId)`.
  2. Calls `authorizeTraffickerAccess` ŌĆö 403 if forbidden (ownership check, audit #13).
  3. Looks up `walletService.getTwoFactorConfig(traffickerId)` (TwoFactorConfig row includes the AES-256-GCM encrypted `secret` + `enabled` flag).
  4. If `cfg?.enabled` is true:
     - missing `totpCode` ŌåÆ 400 `{ error: 'C├│digo 2FA requerido' }`
     - `verifyTOTP(String(totpCode), cfg.secret)` returns false ŌåÆ 400 `{ error: 'C├│digo 2FA inv├Īlido' }`
     - else `totpVerified = true`
  5. If 2FA not enabled ŌåÆ `totpVerified = false` (withdrawal stays `pending_2fa` until admin processes via `/api/wallet action=process_withdrawal`).
  - Replaced the vulnerable `totpVerified = !!totpCode` line (route.ts:357).

**Fix 4 ŌĆö /api/trafficker GET ownership check (P1, financial):**
- `GET` now destructures `session` from `requireAuth()` and calls `authorizeTraffickerAccess(session, trafficker.email)` after the profile is fetched ŌĆö 403 for non-self / non-admin-finance callers.
- POST dispatcher also destructures `session` so it can be passed to `withdraw()`.

**Fix 5 ŌĆö /api/wallet POST `process_withdrawal` role check (P1, financial):**
- POST handler now destructures `session` from `resolveTrafficker(req)`.
- Inside the `process_withdrawal` case, added a role gate: `if (session?.user?.role !== 'admin' && session?.user?.role !== 'finance') return 403 { error: 'Forbidden: admin/finance only' }` ŌĆö runs BEFORE the withdrawal lookup, so non-admin callers can't even probe for valid withdrawalIds.

**Fix 3 ŌĆö /api/payments/config (P0 #1, #2 ŌĆö credential leak + tampering):**
- Re-wrote the entire route. Both GET and PATCH now:
  1. Read `tenantId` from `session.user.tenantId` (not from query/body ŌĆö the route never had one).
  2. Return `{ channels: [], global: {} }` (GET) or 403 (PATCH) for platform admins with no tenantId ŌĆö no global "all tenants" view through this route.
- GET: `db.channel.findMany({ where: { tenantId } })`; `db.setting.findMany()` results filtered to drop `cred::*` rows entirely (those are managed by `/api/integrations/credentials`). Added `maskIfCredential()` belt-and-suspenders guard.
- PATCH: Fetches the channel by `channelId` first, verifies `existing.tenantId === tenantId` (403 on mismatch, 404 if not found) BEFORE update ŌĆö previously any authed user could mutate any channel by id (including `whatsappToken`, `pageAccessToken`, `appSecret`).
- PATCH `fields.global` whitelist: only `roas_kill_threshold` and `cpa_target` keys are accepted. Any `cred::*` key in `fields.global` returns 400. Unknown non-cred keys silently dropped (defense-in-depth, preserves API shape for valid requests).

**Fix 2a ŌĆö tenantId-based routes (15 routes):**
Added a new helper `resolveTenantId(tenantIdParam)` to `src/lib/auth-helpers.ts` that returns `{ session, tenantId, error }`:
- tenant users ŌåÆ pinned to their own tenantId; passing a different one ŌåÆ 403
- platform admins (no tenantId on session) ŌåÆ honour the param or fall through to `undefined` ("all tenants" legacy view)

Applied to:
1. `/api/overview/route.ts` GET ŌĆö `resolveTenantId(query.tenantId)` instead of `requireAuth()`.
2. `/api/orders/route.ts` GET ŌĆö `resolveTenantId`.
3. `/api/conversations/route.ts` GET ŌĆö `resolveTenantId`. POST ŌĆö rewrote: fetch conversation by id first, `requireTenantAccess(conv.tenantId)`, then create the message with `tenantId: conv.tenantId` (killed the hardcoded `'ten-saramantha'` fallback, audit #42).
4. `/api/ads/route.ts` GET ŌĆö `resolveTenantId`. Also filtered the inline `db.setting.findMany()` to drop `cred::*` rows before building the `cfg` object (defense-in-depth against #1/#3).
5. `/api/ads/import/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after schema validation; runs before any external adapter call.
6. `/api/catalog/products/route.ts` GET ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the existing 400 check.
7. `/api/catalog/sync/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the 400 check, before the tenant existence lookup + queue enqueue.
8. `/api/catalog/send-to-chat/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the 400 check, before the service call.
9. `/api/shipping/quote/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the 400 check, before the external adapter call.
10. `/api/shipping/guide/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the 400 check, before the order fetch.
11. `/api/monetization/gmv/route.ts` GET ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the 400 check.
12. `/api/monetization/generate-invoice/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the 400 check, before the invoice upsert.
13. `/api/orchestrate/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` after the action validation, before any LLM call.
14. `/api/agents/[agentName]/route.ts` POST ŌĆö `requireTenantAccess(ctx.tenantId)` after the existing 400 check, before the LLM call. (GET handler kept on `requireAuth()` ŌĆö it returns global agent metadata, no tenant scope.)

**Fix 2b ŌĆö resource-ID routes (5 routes):**
15. `/api/ai-reply/route.ts` POST ŌĆö fetch conversation first, `requireTenantAccess(conv.tenantId)` before the LLM call. Also narrowed `db.product.findMany` to `where: { active: true, tenantId: conv.tenantId }` so the LLM context only includes this tenant's products.
16. `/api/orders/[id]/route.ts` PATCH ŌĆö `requireAuth()` ŌåÆ destructure `session`, fetch `orderService.getOrderById(id)`, 404 if not found, 403 if `session.user.tenantId !== order.tenantId` (and caller is not a platform admin with no tenantId), then call `updateOrder`. Mirrors the `/api/novedades/[id]` `getCaseOrFail()` pattern.
17. `/api/conversations/[id]/route.ts` GET + PATCH ŌĆö added `getConversationOrFail(id)` helper that does `requireAuth()` + `db.conversation.findUnique({ select: { id, tenantId } })` + tenant guard (404 / 403). Used a direct `db.conversation.findUnique` (not `conversationService.getConversationById`) for the guard because the service method clears the unread badge as a side-effect, which we don't want firing before the tenant check passes. After the guard, GET calls the service method (clearing unread now safe); PATCH calls `conversationService.updateStatus`.
18. `/api/ads/[id]/route.ts` PATCH ŌĆö `requireAuth()` ŌåÆ destructure `session`, fetch `db.ad.findUnique({ select: { id, campaign: { select: { tenantId } } } })` (Ad has no direct tenantId column ŌĆö it's scoped via Campaign), 404 if not found, 403 if `session.user.tenantId !== ad.campaign.tenantId`. Then proceeds to `adsService.updateAd`.
19. `/api/channels/route.ts` GET + POST + PATCH + DELETE ŌĆö all four methods now enforce tenant access:
    - GET: requires `tenantId` in query (400 if missing); `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)`; `db.channel.findMany({ where: { tenantId } })`.
    - POST: `requireTenantAccess(body.tenantId)` before the channel create.
    - PATCH: `requireAuth()` + fetch channel by `channelId` + verify `existing.tenantId === session.user.tenantId` (403 on mismatch) before update.
    - DELETE: `requireAuth()` + fetch channel by `channelId` + verify tenant before soft-delete.

**Auth-helpers.ts addition:**
- Added `resolveTenantId(tenantIdParam)` helper for routes where `tenantId` is optional (overview/orders/conversations GET/ads GET). Tenant users are pinned to their own tenantId (cross-tenant attempts ŌåÆ 403); platform admins can pass any tenantId or omit it for the legacy "all tenants" view (only reachable by platform admins because tenant users always have a tenantId on their session).

Stage Summary:

- `src/app/api/trafficker/route.ts` ŌĆö TOTP verification + ownership check on GET + withdraw (P0 #5, P1 #13).

**Verification results:**
- `npx tsc --noEmit` ŌåÆ exit 0 (clean typecheck).
- `bun run lint` ŌåÆ exit 0 (no eslint errors).
- `bunx vitest run` ŌåÆ 180/180 tests pass (10 test files, 0 failures).
- `rg "requireAuth\(\)" src/app/api/ --type ts | wc -l` = 24 lines, of which:
  - 14 are real `requireAuth()` calls ŌĆö all on routes that either (a) do their own tenant guard immediately after (channels PATCH/DELETE, conversations/[id], orders/[id], novedades/[id], ads/[id], trafficker GET + POST, payments/config GET + PATCH, wallet POST), or (b) are intentionally global (tenants list, agents list ├Ś2, base /api health check), or (c) are out of scope for this task (`/api/integrations/credentials` is P0 #3 ŌĆö separate task per worklog; `/api/monetization/commission` was not flagged by the audit).
  - 3 are inside code comments.
  - The remaining lines are import statements / helper-internal calls.

**Notes / out-of-scope items the next task should pick up:**
- `/api/integrations/credentials` (P0 #3) ŌĆö still uses `requireAuth()` and returns masked credentials across ALL tenants. The `Setting` model has no `tenantId` column (per audit) so a proper fix requires a Prisma migration (out of scope per task rules: "DO NOT modify prisma/schema.prisma").
- `/api/monetization/commission` (GET + POST) ŌĆö same insecure pattern as the 19 routes fixed here but not flagged in the audit. Should be patched in a follow-up (GET: `requireTenantAccess(query.tenantId)`; POST: fetch order ŌåÆ verify tenant ŌåÆ upsert).
- TOTP rate-limiting ŌĆö the `withdraw` action is rate-limited via the route's existing 60 req/min cap, but a tighter per-trafficker TOTP-attempt limiter would prevent brute-forcing the 6-digit code (10^6 / 30s window = ~33k attempts needed at 1 window; with rate-limit this becomes impractical, but a per-trafficker 5-attempts-per-5-min lockout would be a strong defense-in-depth).
- Backup-code verification path ŌĆö `verifyBackupCode` exists in `@/lib/totp` but the `withdraw` handler doesn't accept backup codes yet. If a trafficker loses their authenticator, the only path today is admin/finance processing the withdrawal as `pending_2fa` (which is correct ŌĆö backup codes are an admin-recovery concern, not a self-service one).

Task ID: FIX-REALTIME-WEBHOOKS-001
Agent: senior-realtime-engineer
Task: Socket.io auth + tenant isolation + webhook dev-mode strict + health 503 + AI rate limits

Work Log:
- Read worklog tail (lines 5695ŌĆō5845) for context ŌĆö prior AUDIT-OBSERVABILITY-REALTIME-001 found 6 P0/P1/P2 real-time issues (R1 io.emit broadcast, R2 no socket auth, R3 dev-mode signature acceptance, R4 in-memory-only idempotency, O3 health 200-on-error, P2 AI endpoints no per-route rate limit). Baseline confirmed: `bun run lint` exit 0, `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0, `bunx vitest run` 180/180 passing. No idempotency/webhookVerify tests exist (safe to refactor).
- **R1 + R2 ŌĆö Socket.io auth + tenant isolation (server):** Rewrote `mini-services/chat-service/index.ts`. Added `io.use()` JWT verification middleware that reads the token from EITHER `socket.handshake.auth.token` (explicit, native clients) OR `socket.handshake.headers.cookie` (httpOnly NextAuth session cookie auto-sent by the browser via `withCredentials: true`). The JWT is verified with HS256 using Node's `crypto` module (no new dep added ŌĆö chat-service package.json stays `socket.io`-only). Signature comparison uses `crypto.timingSafeEqual` to avoid timing side-channels. In production the middleware rejects connections when `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` is missing (mirrors `src/lib/auth.ts` strictness); in dev it logs a warning and tags the socket with a `dev` tenant so a fresh checkout can still demo. Each verified socket is joined to room `tenant:<tenantId>`. Replaced ALL `io.emit(...)` calls with `io.to(\`tenant:${tenantRoom}\`).emit(...)` ŌĆö confirmed via `rg "io\.emit\b" mini-services/` returning exit 1 (0 matches). `socket.broadcast.emit('agent:typing')` became `socket.to(tenantRoom).emit('agent:typing')` (tenant-scoped, excludes sender). Tightened CORS: `cors: { origin: CHAT_CORS_ORIGIN || CORS_ORIGIN || ['http://localhost:3000', 'http://localhost', 'http://localhost:81'], methods: ['GET', 'POST'], credentials: true }` (no more `'*'`). Also added a lightweight `/health` GET endpoint so the docker-compose healthcheck (`wget --spider http://localhost:3003/health`) actually gets a 200 instead of a 404. Smoke-tested via `bun mini-services/chat-service/index.ts` ŌĆö boots, parses, hits EADDRINUSE (3003 already bound by the dev server), graceful shutdown handler runs cleanly. Bun build: 60 modules bundled in 24ms.
- **R1 + R2 ŌĆö Socket.io client:** Updated `src/lib/socket.ts`. Added `withCredentials: true` (correct socket.io-client option name ŌĆö `credentials` doesn't exist in `ManagerOptions`, caught by tsc on first run). Added optional `token` arg for non-browser clients (passed as `auth.token`). Added `connect_error` listener that logs auth-related errors so the UI can surface a re-login prompt instead of silently retrying forever. Tears down any half-open socket from a previous failed attempt before creating a new one.
- **R3 ŌĆö Webhook dev-mode strict (4 adapters + 2 webhook routes):** Replaced the `return signature.length > 0` fallback in `mercadopago.ts` (line 211), `stripe.ts` (line 237), `wompi.ts` (line 197), `payu.ts` (line 282) with: `if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') throw new Error('...')` + `console.warn` in dev + `return true`. Same pattern for `whatsapp/route.ts` (line 31) and `meta/route.ts` (line 30) ŌĆö they return a 500 with `{ error: 'Webhook secret not configured' }` and audit-log `webhook.{wa,meta}.no_secret` before reaching the `signature.length > 0` fallback. Wrapped each payment webhook route's `adapter.webhookVerify()` call in try/catch so the thrown error becomes a clean 500 + `webhook.<gateway>.config_error` audit row (rather than propagating to Next.js's default 500 handler). The 500 (instead of ACK 200) is deliberate ŌĆö it triggers gateway retries that alert the operator via monitoring. Dev-mode still accepts any non-empty signature so local testing without secrets continues to work.
- **R4 + R12 ŌĆö Idempotency hash collision + multi-instance safety:** Replaced the djb2 32-bit hash in `src/lib/middleware/idempotency.ts` (collision-prone at high webhook volume ŌĆö birthday bound ~65k events) with `crypto.createHash('sha256').update(body + signature).digest('hex')`. Output is `wh_<64 hex chars>` (suitable for `AuditLog.entityId`). Added new `isDuplicateWebhookDB(actionPrefix, webhookId)` async helper that queries `db.auditLog.findFirst({ where: { entityId: webhookId, action: { startsWith: actionPrefix }, createdAt: { gte: now - 10min } } })` ŌĆö works across instances because all share the DB. Returns `false` on DB error (webhook never 500s just because the dedup check failed ŌĆö falls back to in-memory Map). Extended `safeAudit(action, entity, meta, entityId?)` in `payment-webhook-utils.ts` with an optional 4th `entityId` param (backward-compatible ŌĆö existing 3-arg callers still work). Updated all 6 webhook routes (stripe, mercadopago, wompi, payu, whatsapp, meta) to: (1) check the in-memory Map (fast path), (2) check the DB (durable), (3) pass `webhookId` as `entityId` to `safeAudit` so the next retry from ANY instance finds it. The in-memory Map is warmed on DB-hit so the next in-process retry is fast-pathed.
- **O3 ŌĆö Health endpoint 503 on error:** `src/app/api/health/route.ts` GET now returns `503` when `status === 'error'` (DB down, Redis down with `REDIS_URL` set, disk <10% free). `warning` (slow DB, low disk) still returns 200 ŌĆö the app is serving traffic, just degraded. Load balancers and uptime monitors can now drain on 503.
- **P2 ŌĆö Per-route rate limits on AI endpoints:** Added `rateLimit(req, { max: 10, windowMs: 60_000, namespace: 'api:ai-reply' })` to `/api/ai-reply`, `max: 10, namespace: 'api:agents'` to `/api/agents/[agentName]`, and `max: 5, namespace: 'api:orchestrate'` to `/api/orchestrate` (lower because `action='full'` runs 9 LLM calls per request). Each is the first statement in the POST handler ŌĆö before any DB query or LLM call. The global 60/min/IP middleware remains as a backstop.

Stage Summary:

### Files modified (10)
- `mini-services/chat-service/index.ts` ŌĆö JWT auth middleware (HS256 via `crypto`), tenant room join, all `io.emit` ŌåÆ `io.to(tenant:<id>)`, tightened CORS, added `/health` endpoint.
- `src/lib/socket.ts` ŌĆö `withCredentials: true`, optional `auth.token`, `connect_error` listener.
- `src/lib/adapters/mercadopago.ts` ŌĆö `webhookVerify` dev-mode strict (throw in prod, warn+allow in dev).
- `src/lib/adapters/stripe.ts` ŌĆö same.
- `src/lib/adapters/wompi.ts` ŌĆö same.
- `src/lib/adapters/payu.ts` ŌĆö same.
- `src/lib/middleware/idempotency.ts` ŌĆö djb2 ŌåÆ sha256, added `isDuplicateWebhookDB(actionPrefix, webhookId)` async helper.
- `src/lib/adapters/payment-webhook-utils.ts` ŌĆö extended `safeAudit` with optional `entityId` param (backward-compatible).
- `src/app/api/webhooks/{stripe,mercadopago,wompi,payu}/route.ts` ŌĆö try/catch around `webhookVerify` (500 on config error), 2-layer idempotency (in-memory + DB), pass `webhookId` as `entityId` to `safeAudit`.
- `src/app/api/webhooks/{whatsapp,meta}/route.ts` ŌĆö dev-mode strict (500 + audit `no_secret` in prod, warn+allow in dev), 2-layer idempotency, pass `entityId` to `db.auditLog.create`.
- `src/app/api/health/route.ts` ŌĆö return 503 when `status === 'error'`.
- `src/app/api/ai-reply/route.ts`, `src/app/api/orchestrate/route.ts`, `src/app/api/agents/[agentName]/route.ts` ŌĆö per-route rate limits (10/10/5 per min per IP).

### Verification results
- `bun run lint` ŌåÆ exit 0 Ō£ō

- **Idempotency in production multi-instance** ŌĆö the DB-backed `isDuplicateWebhookDB` is durable across instances, but it adds 1 DB round-trip per webhook. For very high-throughput deployments, swap the in-memory Map for `redisSet('idem:'+id, 1, 300)` (the `isDuplicateWebhook()` API stays the same ŌĆö documented in the file header). The DB check remains as a durable fallback.

Real matches: 0. | Cannot receive buyers from ChatGPT/Copilot Instant Checkout. |

Only `kyc_pending` string exists as a `Trafficker.status` enum (not a flow). | No KYC flow, no biometric/document verification, no audit record that would satisfy "carga din├Īmica de la prueba". Blocks any credit/installment feature in Colombia from Nov 2026 onward. |
| 22 | Ley 1581 de 2012 ŌĆö legal basis + revocation for personal data | ┬¦12.2 | ŌØī MISSING | `rg "consent.*revoc\|data.*subject.*request\|DSR\|habeas.*data" src/ --type ts -l` ŌåÆ 0 files. | No DSR endpoint, no consent registry, no legal-basis tagging on PII columns. |

| 26 | Agentic flow ŌĆö Decision (within authorized limits) | ┬¦7 | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ PARTIAL | `speech`, `cart_builder`, `sales_retainer` agents exist. | "Within authorized limits" is the missing piece ŌĆö no Intent Mandate enforces bounds. |
| 27 | Agentic flow ŌĆö Payment (execute with verification) | ┬¦7 | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ PARTIAL | `checkout` + `address` + `address_analysis` agents + 5 payment adapters (Stripe/MP/Wompi/PayU + create-link). | No AP2 Payment Mandate; no cryptographic verification of who authorized the charge. |
| 28 | Agentic flow ŌĆö Fulfillment (ship + track) | ┬¦7 | Ō£ģ COMPLIANT | `logistics`, `guide_tracking`, `novedades`, `redelivery`, `guide_alert`, `logistics_notifier` agents + `/api/shipping/{quote,guide}` + `/api/guide-movements` + `/api/redelivery`. | ŌĆö |
| 29 | Agentic flow ŌĆö Learning (record results) | ┬¦7 | Ō£ģ COMPLIANT | `remarketing`, `customer_score`, `carrier_score`, `affiliator` agents + `BuyerBehavior` + `BehaviorAlert` + `CustomerScore` + `CarrierScore` models. | ŌĆö |
| 30 | Conversions API (CAPI) ŌĆö Meta + Google + TikTok server-side | ┬¦14.4 | Ō£ģ COMPLIANT | `/api/conversions` route + `conversionsService` + `PixelConfig` + `ConversionEvent` + `capi-fire` BullMQ job in `src/lib/queue.ts` with per-platform firing helpers + test mode + pending/sent/failed status tracking. | Only gap: no documented event-mapping config (Purchase/AddToCart/Lead) per platform ŌĆö but the framework is in place. |
| 31 | Architecture layering (data/orchestration/payment/trust/observability/channels) | ┬¦16 | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ PARTIAL | Adapters (`src/lib/adapters/`), services (`src/lib/services/`), orchestrator (`/api/orchestrate`), middleware (`src/lib/middleware/`), agents (`src/lib/agents/`). | Missing dedicated **trust layer** ŌĆö no mandates, no revocation, no consent. |
| 32 | x402 / MPP machine-to-machine payment rails | ┬¦9.1 | ŌØī MISSING | No stablecoin / machine-to-machine payment code. | Optional per document ŌĆö only relevant for B2B micropayments vertical. |

### Critical Gaps (Top 10, prioritized)

**Tier 1 ŌĆö Protocol support (blocks the core thesis)**

1. **AP2 mandate chain (Intent + Cart + Payment) as W3C Verifiable Credentials ŌĆö entirely missing.** This is the cryptographic trust primitive that the entire "comercio ag├®ntico" thesis rests on. Without it, ZIAY cannot prove "the user authorized this purchase" in a non-repudiable way, and cannot integrate with Stripe/Adyen's AP2 dashboard support. **Impact:** Cannot sell to enterprises that require auditable AI purchasing. **Fix size:** New Prisma models (`IntentMandate`, `CartMandate`, `PaymentMandate` with `proof`, `issuer`, `holder`, `credentialSchema`, `revoked` columns) + signing service (ed25519 or JSON Web Signature) + 3 API routes + verification middleware in payment flow.

2. **UCP `/.well-known/ucp` manifest + 4 capabilities ŌĆö entirely missing.** External AI agents (Gemini, ChatGPT) cannot discover ZIAY merchants. **Impact:** Invisible to AI shopping discovery; cannot be a UCP-adopting merchant. **Fix size:** Public Next.js route at `src/app/.well-known/ucp/route.ts` returning the JSON manifest from `Tenant` + `payment-registry`; new `/api/ucp/v1/checkout` endpoint implementing the state machine; new `/api/ucp/v1/identity-linking`, `/order`, `/payment-token-exchange`.

3. **UCP checkout state machine (`incomplete` ŌåÆ `requires_escalation` ŌåÆ `ready_for_complete`) ŌĆö missing.** Current `Order.status` is operational, not protocol-level. **Impact:** Cannot signal to an external agent that a human must take over, and cannot hand back a continuation URL. **Fix size:** New `UcpCheckoutSession` model + 3-state transition logic in a new `ucpCheckout.service.ts`; expose continuation URL in `requires_escalation` response.

4. **ACP (OpenAI/Stripe) support ŌĆö entirely missing.** Blocks any future ChatGPT/Copilot Instant Checkout integration. **Impact:** Cannot receive buyers from the OpenAI ecosystem. **Fix size:** Separate `/.well-known/acp`-style manifest (per OpenAI/Stripe spec) + ACP checkout endpoint. Lower priority than UCP because ACP's in-chat checkout was deprioritized by OpenAI in March 2026 (per document ┬¦9.1).

5. **MCP transport + A2A agent-card.json ŌĆö entirely missing.** Claude/ChatGPT cannot call ZIAY as a native tool; no agent-to-agent discovery. **Impact:** Cannot participate in MCP-native agent ecosystems. **Fix size:** New `/api/mcp` route exposing the 4 UCP capabilities as MCP tool definitions; new `/.well-known/agent-card.json` static route.

**Tier 2 ŌĆö Regulatory compliance (blocks Colombia launch)**

6. **Ley 2573 de 2026 ŌĆö traceable identity verification for credit/installment purchases ŌĆö entirely missing.** General regime enters force November 2026. **Impact:** Cannot offer credit/installments (cuotas) in Colombia without facing "carga din├Īmica de la prueba" ŌĆö customer is exonerated and ZIAY assumes 100% of the loss on any impersonation claim. **Fix size:** `IdentityVerification` model (documentType, documentNumber, verificationMethod, verificationTimestamp, evidenceHash, verifiedBy) + KYC adapter (could integrate Colombian KYC providers) + gate on `paymentMode` Ōłł {credit, installment} routes. AP2 Intent Mandates from gap #1 are explicitly called out in ┬¦12.1 as candidate audit evidence ŌĆö they would partially satisfy this.

7. **Ley 1581 de 2012 ŌĆö legal basis tagging + consent revocation mechanism ŌĆö entirely missing.** Reform bill Aug 2025 raises fines to 5% of annual revenue. **Impact:** Operating on customer PII (preferences, history, saved payment methods) fed to AI agents without documented legal basis or revocation mechanism is a direct violation. **Fix size:** `ConsentRecord` model (customerId, purpose, legalBasis, grantedAt, revokedAt, source) + `/api/consent` CRUD + `/api/dsr` (data subject request) endpoint + per-column legal-basis annotation in schema docs + agent-runtime consent check before reading PII.

8. **Supervisor mapping (SIC vs SFC vs MinTIC) ŌĆö entirely missing.** No documented regulatory mapping. **Impact:** Cannot demonstrate compliance readiness to an acquirer, bank partner, or investor. **Fix size:** Mostly documentation (a `REGULATORY-MAPPING.md` listing each ZIAY flow ŌåÆ responsible supervisor ŌåÆ applicable article), plus an audit-trail tag on `AuditLog.action` indicating which supervisor's record-keeping rule applies.

**Tier 3 ŌĆö Governance features (blocks trust at scale)**

9. **Spend limits per category + per-category permissions + mandate revocation ŌĆö entirely missing.** Document ┬¦11 lists these as the four governance pillars. **Impact:** Customer cannot delegate purchasing with confidence ("buy beauty up to $200, never electronics"); no revocation means a leaked/stolen agent credential cannot be killed without disabling the user. **Fix size:** `SpendLimit` model (tenantId, customerId, category, maxAmount, windowMs) + `Mandate.revokedAt` column + `/api/mandates/[id]/revoke` endpoint + runtime check in `cart_builder` and `checkout` agents. AP2 Intent Mandate (gap #1) is the natural carrier for these limits.

10. **AuditLog upgrade to Verifiable Intent compatibility ŌĆö partial.** Current `AuditLog` is plain DB rows; document ┬¦11 requires "Registro auditable compatible con Verifiable Intent". **Impact:** Audit log would not satisfy Mastercard's Verifiable Intent framework (donated to FIDO Alliance April 2026), so audit evidence would not be portable across the AP2 ecosystem. **Fix size:** Add `proof` (JWS or Data-Integrity proof) + `credentialSchema` columns to `AuditLog`; sign each row at insert time with the tenant's keypair; expose a `/api/audit/[id]/verifiable` endpoint that returns the row as a W3C VC. Reuse the signing service built for gap #1.

### Summary

- **Compliant:** 4 of 32 dimensions (CAPI, Evaluation agents, Fulfillment agents, Learning agents).
- **Partial:** 7 (audit log exists but not VC-compatible; 3 of 6 agentic-flow stages have agents but no protocol; architecture layering missing trust layer; escalation is conversational not protocol-state).
- **Missing:** 21 ŌĆö including the entire AP2/UCP/ACP protocol stack, MCP/A2A transports, mandate revocation, per-category spend limits, Ley 2573 identity verification, Ley 1581 consent revocation, supervisor mapping.
- **Extra:** 0 (no features present that the document didn't mention ŌĆö the project is conversational-commerce first, agentic-protocol zero).

The project is a mature conversational commerce platform (26 agents, 5 payment gateways, 55 Prisma models, CAPI attribution, multi-tenant RBAC, 2-layer webhook idempotency) but has **zero native support** for the three protocols (AP2/UCP/ACP) that the study document identifies as the foundation of "comercio ag├®ntico". The conversational agents cover the 6-stage flow internally but cannot interop with external AI buyers (Gemini, ChatGPT, Copilot, custom agents). Colombia launch with credit/installments is blocked from November 2026 by the absence of Ley 2573 identity-verification infrastructure.

Recommended next task: scope a sprint that ships gap #1 (AP2 mandates) + gap #2 (UCP manifest) + gap #6 (Ley 2573 KYC) together ŌĆö they share the signing-service primitive and unblock both the protocol thesis and the Colombian regulatory launch in one architectural pass.

Task ID: AUDIT-CHANNELS-FINANCE-001
Agent: senior-business-analyst
Task: Audit channels, financial model, market readiness, multi-country expansion vs Comercio Ag├®ntico study

Work Log:
- Read worklog tail (lines 6045ŌĆō6145) for context ŌĆö prior two tasks were tenant-isolation + real-time/webhook hardening (180/180 tests passing, lint/tsc clean).
- Read full study doc `upload/Comercio_Agentico_Estudio_Completo.md` (598 lines, ┬¦1ŌĆō┬¦21). Anchored audit on ┬¦13 (channels), ┬¦14 (financial model), ┬¦14.4 (CAPI/response-time), ┬¦15 (TAM/SAM/SOM), ┬¦17 (MVP), ┬¦18 (LATAM expansion), ┬¦11 (trust & safety).
- **Channel adapters inventory (┬¦13):** Listed `src/lib/adapters/` ŌĆö found `whatsapp-catalog.ts` (317 lines, real Meta Graph API HTTP integration for catalog read + inventory update), `tiktok-ads.ts` (208 lines, real TikTok Ads Manager reporting API), but **NO** `whatsapp-messaging.ts`, `messenger.ts`, `instagram.ts`. The 4-channel UI exists (`channels-manager.tsx`) with full credential forms for WA/Messenger/IG (WABA ID, Phone ID, Token, Page ID, IG Account ID, Verify Token, App Secret) ŌĆö but the adapters that would actually send/receive messages on those channels do not exist.
- **WhatsApp webhook receiver:** `src/app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` ŌĆö HMAC verification (HMAC-SHA256 with `META_APP_SECRET`) + 2-layer idempotency (in-memory Map + DB-backed `isDuplicateWebhookDB`) are properly implemented. **BUT the POST handler does not process inbound messages** ŌĆö line 74 says `// In production: parse entry[].changes[].value.messages, resolve customer by wa_id, upsert conversation on the WhatsApp channel, store message, emit via socket.io. We log to audit for the demo.` Only action is `db.auditLog.create({ action: 'webhook.wa.inbound' })`. No `Message` row is created, no `Conversation` is upserted, no socket.io emission. Same for `webhooks/meta/route.ts` (Messenger + IG inbound).
- **WhatsApp outbound message sending:** `conversationService.sendMessage()` only persists the local `Message` row (line 122 comment: "the actual WhatsApp/Messenger delivery is handled by the channel adapter layer ŌĆö this only persists the local message"). No `POST /{phoneNumberId}/messages` (Cloud API) call exists anywhere in `src/`. `phone_number_id` / `whatsapp cloud api messages` searches return zero matches.
- **Meta Business Agent (┬¦13.1):** Zero references in codebase ŌĆö ZIAY does not leverage Meta's native unified agent (announced June 3, 2026 in the study); it builds its own agent layer over (theoretically) Cloud API. This is a defensible strategic decision per ┬¦13.6 but means ZIAY owns the entire integration cost.
- **CAPI / Conversions API (┬¦14.4):** `src/app/api/conversions/route.ts` + `src/lib/queue.ts` `capi-fire` job ŌĆö real HTTP POST to Meta Graph (`/{pixelId}/events`), GA4 Measurement Protocol, TikTok Events API. PixelConfig + ConversionEvent tables exist. Test-mode supported. **Critical gap: the only caller of `enqueue('capi-fire')` is the explicit `/api/conversions` POST route ŌĆö no caller in the codebase auto-fires a `Purchase` event when a payment webhook marks an order paid** (`applyPaymentUpdate` in `payment-webhook-utils.ts` updates `paymentStatus` + writes `OrderEvent` but does NOT enqueue a CAPI event). No `fetch('/api/conversions')` client-side call exists either. The closed-loop attribution that ┬¦14.4 calls "the highest-impact improvement" is structurally open.
- **CTWA click_id capture:** Order schema has `sourceAdId`, `sourceCampaign`, `sourcePlatform`, `clickId`, `attributedAt` fields (and a separate `Attribution` model with last_click default). BUT ŌĆö because the WhatsApp webhook POST handler does not parse inbound messages, the `ctwa_click_id` payload that Meta sends with each Click-to-WhatsApp lead is **never extracted, never written to `Order.clickId`**. The attribution fields exist in the schema but are never populated from real WA traffic.
- **Financial unit economics (┬¦14):** Audited `overview.service.ts`, `ads/route.ts`, `monetization-view.tsx`, `ads-view.tsx`. Found: AOV Ō£ō (`revenue / ordersCount`, displayed in overview + ads), CPL Ō£ō (`spend / convReported` per ad, displayed), CPA Ō£ō (`totalSpend / ordersCount`, displayed), grossProfit Ō£ō (`revenuePaid - cogs` where cogs = ╬Ż `orderItem.cost ├Ś quantity`), netProfit Ō£ō (`grossProfit - totalSpend`), ROAS/ROI/CVR Ō£ō, commission Ō£ō (4.5%/3.0%/1.75% tranching via `commissionEntry` + `comisionReconocida` two-moment recognition). **Missing:** per-channel cost/spend breakdown (channelSplit in overview only shows revenue/orders per channel, NOT margin or spend), platform fee per message (Oct 1 2026 Meta service-message pricing ŌĆö no field tracks this), channel operational cost (agent + support cost in volume), net contribution margin per channel. The grossProfit/netProfit numbers are tenant-wide, not channel-wide.
- **First response time (┬¦14.4):** `Conversation` schema has no `firstReplyAt`/`responseTimeMs` field. `Message` schema has `direction` + `createdAt` (so TTR could be computed as `min(outbound.createdAt) - min(inbound.createdAt)` per conversation) but **no service/view computes or displays this**. Zero matches for `firstResponse|responseTime|tiempo.*respuesta|ttr|ttfr|sla.*response`. The `PLAN-ENTERPRISE` pilot doc explicitly sets "Tiempo de respuesta: <5s (antes: 2h)" as a pilot success metric, but the codebase cannot measure or report it.
- **TAM/SAM/SOM (┬¦15):** No project-level doc references TAM/SAM/SOM. Only the source study mentions COP 39.4T TAM Q1 2026 / 186.4M transactions / 14.5% value growth. No bottom-up SOM calc per tenant (target accounts ├Ś AOV ├Ś adoption ├Ś margin).
- **MVP roadmap (┬¦17):** No `ROADMAP.md` in project root. Found `upload/PLAN-ENTERPRISE-COMERCIO-AGENTICO.md` with a 3-phase plan: Fase 1 piloto 30 d├Łas (1 marca, 1 canal), Fase 2 escala 5ŌĆō12 semanas (5ŌĆō10 marcas, multi-canal, multi-pa├Łs), Fase 3 enterprise 13ŌĆō24. Aligns reasonably with study ┬¦17 sequence (validate ŌåÆ test ŌåÆ payment ŌåÆ fulfillment ŌåÆ scale ŌåÆ country). But the doc lives in `/upload/` (input folder), not in the repo as a tracked plan ŌĆö it's reference material, not an in-product roadmap.
- **Vertical readiness (┬¦5, ┬¦14):** Read `prisma/seed.ts` ŌĆö 4 tenants: Saramantha (pijamas), Sublimados Majestic (personalizados), Lovely Pijamas, Sue├▒o de Reina (pijamas). Document's recommended high-recurrence verticals: **reposici├│n de insumos, repuestos, consumo corporativo, belleza, farmacia, alimentos**. ZIAY's tenants are **moda (pijamas) + sublimados (personalized gifts)** ŌĆö none overlap with the high-recurrence set. Fashion/pijamas has a seasonal/quarterly replacement cycle, sublimados is episodic (gifts/events). This is a strategic mismatch with the study's recommendation to attack verticals where the agent can drive **automated repurchases**.
- **Pilot infrastructure (┬¦14.5):** `rg "pilot|piloto|experiment|ab.*test|split.*test"` returns zero matches in `src/`. No A/B test framework, no experiment assignment, no cohort table. `Customer.lifetimeValue` Float field exists (and is surfaced in agent prompts for scoring), `CustomerScore` model exists for VIP/tier classification. No retention/churn measurement ŌĆö the matches for "churn|retention" in agent prompts are LLM prompt text, not measured metrics.
- **Multi-country expansion (┬¦18):** `i18n.ts` supports `es-CO` (full), `es-MX` (placeholder, "same as es-CO for now"), `en-US` ŌĆö **no `pt-BR`**. Currency defaults to COP in 6+ adapters (`whatsapp-catalog.ts`, `shopify.ts`, `woocommerce.ts`, `supabase-catalog.ts` all hardcode `currency: 'COP'`); `Order.currency` and `Tenant.currency` default to COP. **No PSE, PIX, OXXO, SPEI** local payment methods ŌĆö only card-based gateways (MercadoPago, Wompi, Stripe, PayU). PayU adapter does have a `paymentCountry: COP ŌåÆ CO, MXN ŌåÆ MX` branch (line 110), so MX is theoretically reachable, but no IVA/ISR tax computation anywhere (`rg "IVA|ISR|tax.*rate|impuesto"` only returns false-positive matches on `WOMPI_PRIVATE_KEY` substring). `Channel.country` and `Order.country` are free-form `String?`, no enum enforcement.
- **Trust & safety (┬¦11):** AuditLog table exists (action/entityId/meta, indexed) Ō£ō. Decision traceability: partial ŌĆö `Message.aiSuggested` + `aiConfidence` flags, no full agent decision log. **Escalation to human**: only as an LLM-decided action in `buyer_behavior.ts` prompt (`escalar_humano` is one of 5 available agent intents), no hard-coded escalation rules (e.g., "if order > $X escalate", "if first-time customer escalate"). The `novedades` module provides a human-review case queue. **Spend limits per category**: zero matches (`rg "spend.*limit|limite.*gasto|budget.*limit"` returns nothing). **Mandate revocation**: zero real matches (only `URL.revokeObjectURL` browser API). **Liability shift / out-of-scope purchase responsibility**: zero matches.

### Business Gaps ŌĆö Top 10

1. **WhatsApp inbound message processing is a stub.** `webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` POST handler only writes raw body to `auditLog` ŌĆö it does NOT parse `entry[].changes[].value.messages`, does NOT upsert `Conversation`/`Message`, does NOT emit via socket.io. Production WhatsApp is non-functional: the agent can never receive a customer message. (Study ┬¦13.1, ┬¦13.6: "WhatsApp: cat├Īlogo navegable + agente de IA con tarea concreta".)

2. **WhatsApp outbound message sending not implemented.** No `POST /{phoneNumberId}/messages` (Cloud API) call anywhere in `src/`. `conversationService.sendMessage()` only persists a local `Message` row. The agent can compose a reply but never actually deliver it to the customer's WhatsApp. Combined with gap #1, the WhatsApp channel is end-to-end broken in production.

3. **CAPI closed-loop attribution is open.** `capi-fire` job exists and posts to Meta/GA4/TikTok, but the only caller is the explicit `POST /api/conversions` route ŌĆö **nothing auto-fires a `Purchase` event when a payment webhook marks an order `paid`**. The study (┬¦14.4) calls this "the highest-impact improvement reported in 2026" for WhatsApp attribution.

4. **CTWA `click_id` capture is missing.** Schema fields exist (`Order.clickId`, `sourceAdId`, `sourceCampaign`, `sourcePlatform`, `Attribution`), but because the WA webhook doesn't parse inbound messages, the `ctwa_click_id` Meta attaches to each Click-to-WhatsApp lead is never extracted or persisted. The attribution infrastructure is built but never fed real data.

5. **First response time (TTR) is not tracked.** No `firstReplyAt` field on `Conversation`; no service computes `min(outbound.createdAt) - min(inbound.createdAt)`. Study ┬¦14.4: "conversion drops >50% if no response in 15ŌĆō30 min". Pilot doc sets `<5s` target. **The single most important WhatsApp metric is unmeasurable today.**

6. **Net contribution margin per channel is not computed.** `channelSplit` shows `{orders, revenue}` per channel but no spend/cost/margin. The central question of ┬¦14 ŌĆö "does WhatsApp pay for itself vs. Instagram?" ŌĆö cannot be answered. Platform message cost (post-Oct 2026), AI token cost, and human-support cost are all untracked.

7. **Vertical strategy mismatches study recommendation.** All 4 ZIAY tenants are moda/pijamas + sublimados ŌĆö neither is in the study's high-recurrence set (reposici├│n, repuestos, consumo corporativo, belleza, farmacia, alimentos). Agent-driven repurchase (the core agentic-commerce value prop) is structurally weak in fashion vs. e.g., pharmacy or B2B supplies.

8. **Multi-country expansion infrastructure is missing.** No multi-currency (COP hardcoded in 6 adapters), no `pt-BR` locale (Brazil blocked), no local payment methods (PSE for CO, PIX for BR, OXXO for MX), no IVA/ISR tax handling. Study ┬¦18 says LATAM is "the next logical layer" ŌĆö the codebase cannot serve any market outside Colombia today.

9. **Trust & safety core requirements are absent.** No spend limits per category, no mandate revocation, no liability definition for out-of-scope purchases ŌĆö all explicitly required by ┬¦11. Human escalation exists only as an LLM-decided intent (`escalar_humano`), not as hard rules. AuditLog table exists but decision traceability is partial (`aiSuggested`/`aiConfidence` flags only).

10. **No pilot/experiment infrastructure.** Zero matches for `pilot|experiment|ab.*test|split.*test` in `src/`. The study (┬¦14.5) says a real financial model requires "CPL propio medido en un piloto de 4ŌĆō6 semanas, tasa de conversi├│n real, margen bruto real por SKU, costo de servicio medido en volumen" ŌĆö none of this can be measured in a controlled way today. LTV/retention/churn exist only as LLM prompt text, not as measured DB metrics.

### Files audited (read-only ŌĆö no files modified)
- `upload/Comercio_Agentico_Estudio_Completo.md` (study, 598 lines)
- `prisma/seed.ts`, `prisma/schema.prisma` (tenant/vertical/financial schema)
- `src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-catalog.ts`, `tiktok-ads.ts`, `payment-registry.ts`, `payment-webhook-utils.ts`
- `src/app/api/webhooks/{whatsapp,meta}/route.ts`, `src/app/api/webhooks/{stripe,mercadopago,wompi,payu}/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/conversions/route.ts`, `src/lib/queue.ts` (CAPI firing)
- `src/lib/services/{overview,conversation,conversions}.service.ts`
- `src/components/dashboard/{overview,ads,monetization,channels-manager}-view.tsx`
- `src/lib/i18n.ts`
- `upload/PLAN-ENTERPRISE-COMERCIO-AGENTICO.md` (roadmap reference)

Task ID: SPRINT-AGENTIC-PROTOCOLS-001
Agent: senior-backend-architect
Task: AP2 mandates + UCP manifest + signing service + Ley 2573/1581 compliance

Work Log:
- Read worklog tail (lines 6148ŌĆō6245) ŌĆö anchored on AUDIT-AGENTIC-PROTOCOLS-001 (14 missing protocol items across ┬¦9ŌĆō┬¦12 of the study doc). Confirmed prior baseline: 180/180 vitest passing, lint/tsc clean, 55-model Prisma schema, zero AP2/UCP/Ley 2573/1581 primitives in code.
- Read study doc ┬¦9 (protocol stack), ┬¦10.1 (UCP manifest + 4 capabilities + state machine), ┬¦10.2 (3-mandate chain as W3C VCs), ┬¦11 (governance checklist: revocation + spend limits + audit), ┬¦12.1 (Ley 2573 ŌĆö carga din├Īmica de la prueba), ┬¦12.2 (Ley 1581 ŌĆö legal basis + revocation).
- **Schema:** Appended 4 new Prisma models at end of `prisma/schema.prisma`: `AP2Mandate` (3-type chain: intent|cart|payment, with parentMandateId self-relation `MandateChain`, W3C VC payload + ed25519 signature + revocation columns + Intent-specific maxAmount/categoryLimits/expiresAt), `UcpCheckoutSession` (state machine incompleteŌåÆrequires_escalationŌåÆready_for_completeŌåÆcompleted/failed, continuation URL, negotiated caps, sessionId @unique for external agents), `IdentityVerification` (Ley 2573 KYC: method/provider/status/expiresAt/evidenceHash/triggerType+riskScore, 4 indexes), `ConsentRecord` (Ley 1581: purpose/legalBasis/granted/revokedAt/proofHash+proofPayload, 3 indexes). Added 4 reverse relations to `Tenant` (ap2Mandates, ucpCheckoutSessions, identityVerifications, consentRecords). All indexes follow existing convention (composite where query patterns demand it). `bunx prisma validate` ŌåÆ valid ­¤ÜĆ; `bun run db:push` ŌåÆ DB in sync; Prisma client regenerated.
- **Signing service** (`src/lib/crypto/signing.ts`, 173 lines): ed25519 keypair management via `crypto.generateKeyPairSync('ed25519')`, PEM stored in `Setting` (keys `cred::signing::{tenantId}::private|public`). Functions: `getOrCreateTenantKeypair` (idempotent, dev-only ŌĆö KMS in prod), `getTenantPublicKey` (verify-only), `createW3CVC` (builds unsigned VC with `@context` + `VerifiableCredential` type + custom subtypes), `signVC` (detached proof over canonical JSON, `Ed25519Signature2020` proof type, base64url), `verifyVC` (returns false on missing proof or invalid signature), `computeHash` (SHA-256 hex), `computeIntentCartHash` (lexicographic-ordered hash linking Intent + Cart for Payment Mandate non-repudiation).
- **UCP manifest** (`src/app/.well-known/ucp/route.ts`, `force-static`): returns the full UCP JSON manifest ŌĆö version `2026-04-08`, 4 capabilities (`dev.ucp.shopping.checkout`, `dev.ucp.common.identity_linking`, `dev.ucp.shopping.order`, `dev.ucp.shopping.payment_token_exchange`), 4 payment handlers (MercadoPago, Wompi, Stripe, PayU) with `allowed_card_networks` configs, REST transport endpoint `/api/ucp/v1`. Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600; CORS: `*`.
- **Middleware update** (`src/middleware.ts`): added `/.well-known/ucp` to `PUBLIC_PATTERNS` so the manifest is accessible without auth (study ┬¦10.1: "debe ser p├║blicamente accesible y no requerir ninguna autenticaci├│n"). All other `/api/**` routes remain auth-gated + rate-limited (60 req/min/IP).
- **AP2 mandate endpoints** (5 routes):
  - `POST /api/ap2/mandates` ŌĆö creates Intent Mandate. Zod-validated body (userId, purpose, maxAmount, currency, categoryLimits?, expiresAt?). Signs W3C VC with tenant private key, stores VC payload + signature + signatoryDid + Intent bounds.
  - `POST /api/ap2/mandates/cart` ŌĆö creates Cart Mandate linked to Intent. Verifies Intent is active + not expired + signature valid + cart total Ōēż maxAmount + per-category caps respected. Signs Cart VC including `totalHash` (deterministic sha256 over sorted items + totals).
  - `POST /api/ap2/mandates/payment` ŌĆö creates Payment Mandate linked to Cart. Verifies Cart active + signature + Intent padre active. Includes `intentCartHash = sha256(sort(intentId, cartId))` in VC subject for non-repudiation. Stores `tokenRef = sha256(paymentToken)` (no PAN).
  - `PATCH /api/ap2/mandates/[id]/revoke` ŌĆö revokes mandate + cascades to all child mandates (BFS over MandateChain relation, depth 3). Sets status=revoked, revokedAt, revokedReason. Idempotent (already-revoked returns 409). Study ┬¦11: "Mandatos revocables en cualquier momento".
  - `GET /api/ap2/mandates/[id]` ŌĆö returns mandate + parsed VC + signature verification status (re-verifies against tenant public key at read time).
  - `GET /api/ap2/mandates?tenantId=X&userId=Y&type=intent&status=active` ŌĆö list with filters.
  - `PATCH /api/ap2/mandates/[id]` ŌĆö advance active ŌåÆ consumed, update orderId/paymentRef.
- **UCP v1 endpoints** (4 capabilities):
  - `POST /api/ucp/v1/checkout` ŌĆö starts session. Negotiates capabilities (intersect agent's declared caps with tenant's 4 caps) + payment handlers (intersect with tenant's 4 handlers). Returns sessionId (UUID), state=incomplete, negotiated result, expiresAt (30 min). 422 if agent lacks `dev.ucp.shopping.checkout`.
  - `GET /api/ucp/v1/checkout/[sessionId]` ŌĆö returns state + cart + continuationUrl + negotiatedCaps + linked mandate IDs + orderId.
  - `PATCH /api/ucp/v1/checkout/[sessionId]` ŌĆö advances state machine. Three transitions: (1) `ŌåÆ requires_escalation` requires continuationUrl; (2) `ŌåÆ ready_for_complete` verifies Intent + Cart Mandate signatures, validates cart.parentMandateId === intent.id, and if paymentMode Ōłł {credit, installment} calls `requireIdentityVerification()` (Ley 2573 gate) ŌĆö if KYC fails, forces `requires_escalation` with continuation URL `/compliance/kyc?verificationId=...`; (3) `ŌåÆ completed` creates a real `Order` row (number `UCP-{sessionid-prefix}`, paymentMode advance|cod|hybrid|credit|installment, origen=`ucp_agent`), creates `OrderItem` rows for matching SKUs, writes `OrderEvent {type: 'created'}`, marks Intent+Cart+Payment Mandates as `consumed` with `orderId`, validates Payment Mandate's `intentCartHash` matches expected.
  - `POST /api/ucp/v1/identity-linking` ŌĆö OAuth-style agentŌåöcustomer linking. Verifies agent's signature over `${agentDid}:${customerId}:${tenantId}:${ts}` (ed25519 or RSA-SHA256), anti-replay (5 min window), creates `IdentityVerification {status: 'verified', triggerRef: linkingToken, expiresAt: +24h}`, returns `linkingToken` UUID.
  - `GET /api/ucp/v1/order/[orderId]` ŌĆö returns order details in UCP format: items, events, shipments (proveedor/numeroGuia/estado), `ucpFulfillmentStatus` mapped from operational status.
  - `POST /api/ucp/v1/payment-token-exchange` ŌĆö exchanges agent's payment token for chargeable instrument. Validates Payment Mandate active + Cart padre active + declared handler matches + `tokenRef = sha256(paymentToken)` matches (no PAN stored). Returns deterministic `paymentRef` (real adapter call in prod), updates mandate with paymentRef.
- **KYC gate** (`src/lib/compliance/kyc-gate.ts`, 152 lines): `requireIdentityVerification(tenantId, userId, triggerType, triggerRef, orderAmount?)` ŌĆö returns `{verified, verificationId, reason}`. Policy: `credit_purchase` + `installment_plan` ŌåÆ ALWAYS require KYC; `high_value_order` ŌåÆ requires if amount > COP 2,000,000. Idempotent: returns existing verified KYC if non-expired, else reuses pending verification for same trigger, else creates new pending. KYC TTL: 90 days. `recordIdentityVerification(id, status, evidenceHash, riskScore?)` ŌĆö marks verified (sets expiresAt = +90d) or failed. `getActiveVerification(tenantId, userId)` ŌĆö read-only check.
- **KYC endpoints** (Ley 2573):
  - `POST /api/compliance/kyc` ŌĆö initiate. Returns 200 {verified: true} if existing valid KYC, else 202 {verified: false, verificationId, reason}.
  - `GET /api/compliance/kyc?tenantId=X&userId=Y` ŌĆö check active verification status.
  - `POST /api/compliance/kyc/[id]/verify` ŌĆö complete (provider webhook or 2FA TOTP). Body: {status: 'verified'|'failed', evidenceHash, riskScore?, provider?}. Updates record, sets expiresAt.
- **Consent + DSR endpoints** (Ley 1581):
  - `POST /api/compliance/consent` ŌĆö record consent with Zod-validated {tenantId, dataSubjectId, dataSubjectType ('customer'|'user'|'lead'), purpose ('marketing'|'analytics'|'ai_processing'|'data_sharing'), legalBasis ('consent'|'contract'|'legitimate_interest'|'legal_obligation'), proofPayload?}. Computes `proofHash = sha256(JSON.stringify(proofPayload))`.
  - `GET /api/compliance/consent?tenantId=X&dataSubjectId=Y` ŌĆö list consents for a data subject.
  - `DELETE /api/compliance/consent?id=Z&reason=...` ŌĆö revoke (granted=false, revokedAt=now).
  - `POST /api/compliance/dsr` ŌĆö Data Subject Request. Three types: (1) `access` ŌĆö returns full bundle: customer + orders + conversations + messages + consents + identityVerifications; (2) `portability` ŌĆö same bundle, format=json-portable; (3) `erasure` ŌĆö anonymizes customer (PII ŌåÆ null/`[anonimizado]`), deletes messages, bulk-revokes all consents for the data subject. Preserves referential integrity (Orders/Shipments kept, only PII wiped).

- Gap #4 (UCP profile negotiation ŌĆö agent Ōł® commerce per-transaction) Ō£ģ

- Gap #10 (AP2 mandate revocation ŌĆö any time, with cascade to children) Ō£ģ

- Gap #Ley 2573 (traceable identity verification for credit/installments) Ō£ģ

- MCP transport + A2A agent-card.json ŌĆö gap #5 + #14 ŌĆö `/api/mcp` + `/.well-known/agent-card.json`.
- AuditLog upgrade to Verifiable Intent (gap #10 in audit) ŌĆö `proof` + `credentialSchema` columns on `AuditLog` + `/api/audit/[id]/verifiable` endpoint. Reuses the signing service built here.
- Supervisor mapping (SIC vs SFC vs MinTIC) ŌĆö gap #23 ŌĆö `REGULATORY-MAPPING.md` + AuditLog.tag.
- Frontend ŌĆö UCP/KYC/Consent admin views (deferred to frontend sprint; rules prohibited touching `src/components/`).

### Recommended next sprint

Build the agent runtime layer on top of this protocol stack:
1. MCP tool exposure (`/api/mcp` exposing the 4 UCP capabilities as MCP tool definitions for Claude/ChatGPT).
2. A2A `/.well-known/agent-card.json` for inter-agent discovery.
3. AuditLog Verifiable Intent upgrade ŌĆö sign each row with tenant keypair, expose as W3C VC.
4. Wire the existing `checkout` + `cart_builder` agents to issue AP2 Intent Mandates instead of plain ConversationalCart rows (backward-compat shim: ConversationalCart ŌåÆ AP2 Intent when cart is locked).
5. Wire payment webhooks (Stripe/MP/Wompi/PayU) to mark AP2 Payment Mandates as `consumed` on `payment_status=paid`.

Task ID: SPRINT-WHATSAPP-FUNCTIONAL-001
Agent: senior-backend-engineer
Task: WhatsApp Cloud API send+receive + CAPI auto-fire + CTWA + TTR

Work Log:
- Read worklog tail (audit `AUDIT-CHANNELS-FINANCE-001` found WhatsApp is a stub: webhook only writes raw body to AuditLog; no message sending; CAPI never auto-fires on paid; CTWA click_id never captured; TTR unmeasurable).
- Read `prisma/schema.prisma`, `src/app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` (stub), `src/lib/services/conversation.service.ts` (sendMessage only persists locally), `src/lib/adapters/payment-webhook-utils.ts` (applyPaymentUpdate never fires CAPI), `src/lib/queue.ts` (`capi-fire` worker exists, posts to Meta/GA4/TikTok), `src/app/api/conversions/route.ts` (only explicit caller of capi-fire), `mini-services/chat-service/index.ts` (socket.io server on :3003, no /emit endpoint), `src/lib/middleware/hmac.ts` + `idempotency.ts` (existing 2-layer dedup to preserve).
- Verified `ConversionEvent` schema uses `pixelConfigId`/`eventType`/`value`/`currency`/`status`/`response` (no `eventId`/`eventTime`/`eventName` columns) ŌĆö adapted the capi-auto-fire module to use the real schema fields and store attribution metadata as JSON in `response`.
- Schema changes (`prisma/schema.prisma`):
  * `Conversation`: added `clickId String?`, `customerPhone String?`, `firstReplyAt DateTime?` + composite index `(tenantId, customerPhone, status)` for the WA webhook's open-conversation lookup by E.164 phone.
  * `Message`: added `waMessageId String?` + index for inbound webhook dedup (Meta retries up to ~24h) and `markMessageRead` calls.
- Created `src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-cloud.ts`:
  * `WhatsAppCloudAdapter` class: `sendMessage` (POST `/{phoneNumberId}/messages`), `sendText` (convenience), `markMessageRead` (best-effort), `isConfigured`.
  * `getWhatsAppAdapter(tenantId)` factory: lazy-loads credentials from the tenant's active WhatsApp `Channel` record (returns null when unconfigured).
  * `findWhatsAppChannelByPhoneNumberId(phoneNumberId)`: reverse lookup used by the webhook to resolve which tenant owns an inbound message (via `value.metadata.phone_number_id`).
- Created `src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-parser.ts`:
  * `parseWhatsAppInbound(payload)`: pure function extracting `{ from, fromName, messageId, timestamp, timestampMs, type, text, textBody, mediaId, caption, imageUrl, location, buttonReply, ctwClickId, referralSourceUrl, phoneNumberId, displayPhoneNumber, contactWaId }`.
  * Handles: text, button, interactive (button_reply + list_reply), image, audio, document, location, unknown.
  * `extractClickIdFromUrl(url)`: parses `?cta_id=` (CTWA v2) and `?ms_id=` (CTWA v1) from `context.cta_url` / `context.referral.source_url`.
  * `context.referral.ctwa_click_id` is preferred (Meta's canonical field) ŌĆö falls back to URL parsing.
- Rewrote `src/app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` POST handler:
  * Preserved: HMAC verification (with dev-mode fallback + production 500 when META_APP_SECRET missing), 2-layer idempotency (in-memory Map + DB-backed AuditLog), GET verification handshake.
  * Added: 3rd idempotency layer on `waMessageId` (DB lookup before persisting ŌĆö Meta re-signs payloads on retry, so the webhookId hash isn't sufficient).
  * Pipeline: parse ŌåÆ resolve tenant via `phone_number_id` ŌåÆ Channel lookup ŌåÆ fallback to env `WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID` ŌåÆ resolve/create Customer by phone ŌåÆ resolve/create open Conversation (stamps `clickId` at creation OR retroactively when first CTWA-bearing message arrives) ŌåÆ persist Message (direction=inbound, waMessageId) ŌåÆ bump conversation `lastMessageAt` + `unreadCount++` ŌåÆ fire-and-forget `emitToTenant('message:new', payload)` + `emitToTenant('message:received', {...})` to chat-service /emit ŌåÆ best-effort `markMessageRead`.
  * Returns 200 ALWAYS (Meta retries on non-200) with `{ received: true, status: 'processed' | 'duplicate' | 'duplicate_message_id' | 'non_message' | 'no_channel' | 'processing_failed' }`.
  * Non-message payloads (status updates, template callbacks) still get an AuditLog row for traceability + ACK 200.
- Created `src/lib/chat-emit.ts`:
  * `emitToTenant(tenantId, event, payload)`: fire-and-forget POST to `http://localhost:3003/emit` (3s timeout). Failures are swallowed + logged ŌĆö the webhook must never block on the realtime fan-out.
  * `CHAT_SERVICE_INTERNAL_URL` env var (defaults to `http://localhost:3003`).
- Added `/emit` POST endpoint to `mini-services/chat-service/index.ts`:
  * Accepts `{ tenantId, event, payload }`, broadcasts via `io.to('tenant:<tenantId>').emit(event, payload)`.
  * Unauthenticated by design ŌĆö only the Next.js process can reach it via localhost; Caddy doesn't proxy `/emit` externally.
  * Health endpoint (`GET /health`) preserved.
- Created `src/lib/attribution/capi-auto-fire.ts`:
  * `fireCapiPurchaseEvent(orderId, tenantId)`: loads order + items + customer, finds active PixelConfigs, pre-creates one `ConversionEvent` row per pixel in 'pending' status (with `eventType='Purchase'`, `value=order.total`, `currency=order.currency`), enqueues the existing `capi-fire` BullMQ job with `{tenantId, eventType, value, currency, pixels, eventIds}` (same shape as `/api/conversions` POST).
  * Attribution metadata (orderId, clickId, sourceAdId, sourceCampaign, sourcePlatform, customerEmailHash, customerPhoneHash) stored as JSON in `ConversionEvent.response` ŌĆö no schema change needed.
  * `hashPii(value)`: SHA-256 lowercase hex (Meta CAPI spec).
  * Best-effort + non-blocking: own try/catch swallows all errors so a CAPI failure never blocks the payment webhook. Skips $0 orders (test data). Skips tenants with no active pixels.
- Hooked `applyPaymentUpdate` (`src/lib/adapters/payment-webhook-utils.ts`) to call `fireCapiPurchaseEvent` when the order transitions to `paid` (condition: `shouldMarkPaid && !wasAlreadyPaid` ŌĆö fires only on the webhook that actually marks it paid, NOT on idempotent retries). Fire-and-forget (`.catch(...)` swallowed).
- Created `src/lib/metrics/ttr.ts`:
  * `recordFirstReply(conversationId)`: idempotent ŌĆö sets `firstReplyAt` only on the first outbound reply.
  * `calculateTtrMinutes(createdAt, firstReplyAt)`: minutes between creation + first reply (null when no reply yet; clamps clock skew to 0).
  * `getTtrStats(tenantId, days=14)`: aggregates avg / median / withinTargetPct (% replied within 5 min, the pilot target) / totalConversations / repliedConversations. Returns nulls when no conversations.
- Updated `src/lib/services/conversation.service.ts` `sendMessage`:
  * Persists the local Message row FIRST (agent sees their reply immediately even if WA delivery is slow/fails).
  * When `conv.channel.type === 'whatsapp'` AND outbound: calls `getWhatsAppAdapter(tenantId)` ŌåÆ `adapter.sendText(customerPhone, body)` ŌåÆ updates the Message row with the WA message ID Meta echoes back. On delivery failure: marks the local row `status='failed'` (captured + logged, not rethrown).
  * Calls `recordFirstReply(conversationId)` after every outbound (idempotent ŌĆö first call wins, subsequent calls are no-ops).
- Updated `src/app/api/conversations/route.ts` POST:
  * Migrated from direct `db.message.create` + `db.conversation.update` to `conversationService.sendMessage(...)` so WA delivery + TTR stamping fire automatically.
  * Added fire-and-forget `emitToTenant('message:new', ...)` so other dashboards of the tenant see the agent's reply in real time.
- Updated `src/app/api/ucp/v1/checkout/[sessionId]/route.ts` PATCH (completed transition):
  * Extended `PatchSchema` with optional `conversationId`, `clickId`, `sourceAdId`, `sourceCampaign`, `sourcePlatform` (Zod-validated).
  * When `conversationId` is supplied: loads the conversation, verifies tenant match (defense-in-depth), inherits `clickId`/`sourceAdId`/`sourceCampaign` from it (explicit body fields take precedence ŌĆö useful for web checkout with client-side click_id).
  * Stamps `Order.conversationId`, `clickId`, `sourceAdId`, `sourceCampaign`, `sourcePlatform`, `attributedAt` on the new Order.
- Extended `CrearPedidoInput` (`src/lib/adapters/ecommerce-adapter.ts`) with optional `conversationId` / `clickId` / `sourceAdId` / `sourceCampaign` / `sourcePlatform` for the WhatsApp Catalog adapter's `crearPedido` path.
- Updated `WhatsappCatalogAdapter.crearPedido` (`src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-catalog.ts`) to stamp `conversationId` + CTWA attribution fields on the new Order when supplied by the calling agent (closes the WA ŌåÆ order ŌåÆ CAPI loop).
- Verification:
  * `bunx prisma validate` ŌåÆ valid Ō£ō
  * `bun run db:push` ŌåÆ applied (Conversation + Message schema additions; Prisma Client regenerated) Ō£ō
  * `bun run lint` (eslint .) ŌåÆ exit 0 Ō£ō
  * `npx tsc --noEmit` ŌåÆ exit 0 Ō£ō
  * `bun run test` (vitest) ŌåÆ 10 files / 180 tests pass Ō£ō
  * `test -f src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-cloud.ts` ŌåÆ OK Ō£ō
  * `test -f src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-parser.ts` ŌåÆ OK Ō£ō
  * `grep parseWhatsAppInbound src/app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` ŌåÆ 4 matches (import + 3 comment/call references) Ō£ō
- Did NOT touch: `src/components/**` (frontend scope), test files, the GET webhook verification handshake, the existing HMAC verification logic, the 2-layer idempotency (in-memory Map + DB AuditLog) ŌĆö all preserved verbatim.

Stage Summary:
- Files created (5):
  * `src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-cloud.ts` ŌĆö Cloud API adapter (sendMessage / sendText / markMessageRead + factory + phone_number_id reverse lookup).
  * `src/lib/adapters/whatsapp-parser.ts` ŌĆö pure inbound payload parser (text/button/interactive/image/audio/document/location + CTWA click_id extraction).

  * `src/app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` ŌĆö rewrote POST to parse inbound, resolve tenant via phone_number_id, upsert Customer/Conversation/Message, stamp CTWA clickId, emit socket events, mark read; preserved HMAC + 2-layer idempotency; added 3rd idempotency layer on waMessageId.
  * `src/lib/adapters/payment-webhook-utils.ts` ŌĆö applyPaymentUpdate now auto-fires CAPI Purchase on transition to paid (best-effort, non-blocking, idempotent on `wasAlreadyPaid`).
  * `src/lib/services/conversation.service.ts` ŌĆö sendMessage now delivers via WhatsApp Cloud API when channel is whatsapp + records TTR.
  * `src/app/api/conversations/route.ts` ŌĆö POST now routes through conversationService.sendMessage + emits socket event.
  * `src/app/api/ucp/v1/checkout/[sessionId]/route.ts` ŌĆö Zod schema + Order creation now inherit clickId/sourceAdId from conversation (CTWA closed-loop).

TTR stats available via `getTtrStats(tenantId)`.

Task ID: SPRINT-FINANCE-META-001
Agent: senior-fintech-ops-engineer
Task: Channel contribution margin (study ┬¦14.1) + Meta Business Agent decision (study ┬¦13.1)

Work Log:
- Read worklog tail (latest sprint SPRINT-WHATSAPP-FUNCTIONAL-001 wired WA Cloud API send/receive + CAPI auto-fire + TTR tracking; `recordFirstReply()` already called from `conversationService.sendMessage`).
- Read `prisma/schema.prisma` (66 models, 1454 lines), `src/lib/services/conversation.service.ts` (TTR already wired at lines 233-238 ŌĆö verified, no change needed), `src/lib/auth-helpers.ts` (`requireTenantAccess` + `requireRole` patterns), `src/app/api/conversions/route.ts` + `src/app/api/compliance/kyc/route.ts` (Zod + Spanish error message patterns), `.env.example` (didn't exist ŌĆö created).

Schema changes (`prisma/schema.prisma`):
  * Added `ChannelCost` model (67th model, +36 lines) ŌĆö study ┬¦14.1 "costo operativo del canal (mensajer├Ła, agente de IA, log├Łstica)".
    Fields: messageCost / aiTokenCost / adSpend / supportCost / logisticsCost / paymentFee (cost breakdown) + revenue / ordersCount (revenue) + netContribution / marginPct (denormalized computed) + `@@unique([tenantId, channel, date])` + `@@index([tenantId, channel, date])`.
    NOTE: dropped `@db.Date` from the spec ŌĆö SQLite (dev provider) doesn't support native `@db.Date` and the existing schema uses ZERO `@db.*` modifiers for portability. The `date` column is `DateTime` stored at 00:00:00.000 local; on PostgreSQL prod, `@db.Date` could be re-added later without breaking the service code.
  * Added reverse relation `channelCosts ChannelCost[]` to `Tenant` (after `consentRecords`).
- Verification: `bunx prisma validate` ŌåÆ valid ­¤ÜĆ; `bun run db:push` ŌåÆ applied (Prisma Client v6.19.2 regenerated).

Files created (5):

1. `src/lib/services/channel-cost.service.ts` ŌĆö Channel contribution margin service.
   * `ChannelContribution` interface (channel, revenue, 6 cost components, totalCost, netContribution, marginPct, ordersCount, aov, cac, cpl).
   * `getChannelContributions(tenantId, startDate, endDate)` ŌĆö groups `ChannelCost` rows by channel over an inclusive date window; sums each cost component; computes totalCost = ╬Ż(components), netContribution = revenue ŌłÆ totalCost, marginPct = netContribution / revenue ├Ś 100, aov = revenue / ordersCount, cac = cpl = adSpend / ordersCount (proxied until a dedicated `Lead` table exists). All values rounded to 2 decimals for tidy dashboard rendering.
   * `recordDailyChannelCosts(tenantId, date)` ŌĆö daily cron job. Normalizes `date` to start-of-day (copy-first, does NOT mutate caller's Date), then for each of the 4 tracked channels (`whatsapp | messenger | instagram | tiktok`): query `Channel.findMany({ where: { tenantId, type: channel } })` to resolve channel IDs (the `Order` model exposes only `channelId` String ŌĆö NO Prisma relation back to `Channel`), then `db.order.findMany({ where: { tenantId, channelId: { in: channelIds }, createdAt: { gte: startOfDay, lt: startOfNextDay } } })` (half-open `[startOfDay, startOfNextDay)` window avoids 23:59:59.999 off-by-one). Cost estimates: messageCost = ordersCount ├Ś $0.0085, aiTokenCost = ordersCount ├Ś $0.02, logisticsCost = ordersCount ├Ś $2.50, paymentFee = revenue ├Ś 2.9% + $0.30. Upserts a single `ChannelCost` row per channel keyed by `(tenantId, channel, date)` ŌĆö idempotent. Per-channel try/catch so a failure on one channel doesn't abort the others.
   * Uses `getLogger` + `captureError` for consistency with the rest of the service layer. Spanish error message: `'No se pudo obtener el margen de contribuci├│n por canal'`.

2. `src/app/api/finance/channel-contribution/route.ts` ŌĆö GET endpoint.
   * Query: `?tenantId=X&startDate=YYYY-MM-DD&endDate=YYYY-MM-DD`.
   * Strict `YYYY-MM-DD` regex parsing + roll-over sanity check (rejects `2026-13-40`). `startDate > endDate` ŌåÆ 400.
   * Protected by `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)`.
   * Returns `{ tenantId, startDate, endDate, channels: ChannelContribution[] }`.
   * Spanish error messages: `'tenantId es requerido'`, `'startDate y endDate son requeridos (formato YYYY-MM-DD)'`, etc.

3. `src/app/api/finance/channel-cost/sync/route.ts` ŌĆö POST endpoint (manual trigger / cron).
   * Body: `{ tenantId: string, date?: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }` (default date = today).
   * Zod-validated (`SyncSchema`).
   * Protected by `requireTenantAccess(tenantId)` + admin role gate (the sync is a destructive upsert ŌĆö overwrites the day's row ŌĆö so it's gated tighter than the read-only contribution endpoint).
   * Returns `{ ok: true, tenantId, date, channels: 4 }` on success.
   * Spanish error messages: `'Cuerpo JSON inv├Īlido'`, `'Par├Īmetros inv├Īlidos'`, `'Forbidden: se requiere rol admin para disparar la sincronizaci├│n'`, `'date no es una fecha de calendario v├Īlida'`.

4. `src/lib/config/meta-agent-config.ts` ŌĆö Meta Business Agent decision framework (study ┬¦13.1).
   * `MetaAgentStrategy = 'meta_native' | 'own_stack' | 'hybrid'`.
   * `MetaAgentConfig` interface (strategy, rationale, 4 feature flags, cost model, 2 data-sharing flags).
   * `META_AGENT_STRATEGIES` ŌĆö 3 fully-specified configs with rationale strings.
   * `getMetaAgentStrategy()` ŌĆö resolves the active strategy from `process.env.META_AGENT_STRATEGY`; falls back to `'own_stack'` (the documented decision) when missing or invalid. Invalid values log a `console.warn` but never throw ŌĆö the conversation flow must not crash on a config typo.
   * `shouldEscalateToOwnAgent({ intent, orderValue?, customerTier? })` ŌĆö routing decision. `own_stack` ŌåÆ always true; `meta_native` ŌåÆ always false; `hybrid` ŌåÆ escalate checkout / novedad / complaint / orderValue > $500k COP / VIP customers to ZIAY's own agents, leave FAQ + catalog_query with Meta.

5. `docs/META-AGENT-DECISION.md` ŌĆö Strategic decision document (study ┬¦13.1).
   * Context (Meta Business Agent launched 2026-06-03).
   * 3 options evaluated (Meta Native / Stack Propio / H├Łbrido) with pros/cons/cost-estimate/data-ceded table.
   * Decision: `own_stack` with 4-point rationale (ZIAY has 26 agents / 100x cheaper / AP2 compliance / infrastructure-thesis).
   * "Cu├Īndo reconsiderar" triggers.
   * Routing-logic table for hybrid mode (intent ├Ś handler matrix).
   * Implementation references (`meta-agent-config.ts`, `.env.example`, `ChannelCost.aiTokenCost`).

6. `.env.example` ŌĆö created (didn't exist before). Contains the `META_AGENT_STRATEGY=own_stack` block with 4-line comment explaining the 3 options. (Note: `.gitignore` ignores `.env*` so this file is untracked ŌĆö that's intentional; it serves as the deploy-time template.)

Verification (Step 7 ŌĆö `recordFirstReply` already wired):
  * `grep -n "recordFirstReply" src/lib/services/conversation.service.ts` ŌåÆ 2 matches (import line 16 + call at line 237 inside `sendMessage` outbound branch). No change needed ŌĆö Sprint B (SPRINT-WHATSAPP-FUNCTIONAL-001) already wired this. Confirmed in worklog tail: "Calls `recordFirstReply(conversationId)` after every outbound (idempotent ŌĆö first call wins, subsequent calls are no-ops)".

Verification results:
| Command | Result |
|---------|--------|
| `bunx prisma validate` | Ō£ģ valid ­¤ÜĆ |
| `bun run db:push` | Ō£ģ Database is now in sync; Prisma Client v6.19.2 generated |
| `bun run lint` (eslint .) | Ō£ģ exit 0 (0 errors, 0 warnings) |
| `npx tsc --noEmit` | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ 3 errors ŌĆö ALL in files created by parallel agents in this session (`src/app/api/governance/liability/route.ts:121` duplicate property, `src/lib/adapters/local-payments.ts:269` Record-type conversion, `src/lib/crypto/audit-signing.ts:83` `credentialSchema` not in `W3CVerifiableCredential`). Verified `git ls-files --error-unmatch` returns "did not match any file(s) known to git" for all 3 ŌåÆ all UNTRACKED, none from this sprint's changes. `grep -E "(finance|channel-cost|meta-agent)"` on tsc output ŌåÆ no matches ŌåÆ my files are clean. |
| `bunx vitest run` | Ō£ģ 10 test files / 180/180 tests passing |

Endpoint count:
- 2 new HTTP endpoints:
  * `GET /api/finance/channel-contribution?tenantId=X&startDate=YYYY-MM-DD&endDate=YYYY-MM-DD` ŌĆö read-only channel contribution report (study ┬¦14.1).
  * `POST /api/finance/channel-cost/sync` ŌĆö manual/cron trigger for daily cost backfill (admin-only).
- 0 changes to existing endpoints.

Stage Summary:
- Channel contribution margin is now measurable end-to-end: revenue (from `Order.total` summed by `channelId ŌåÆ Channel.type`) ŌłÆ 6 cost components (message / AI token / ad spend / support / logistics / payment fee) = netContribution; marginPct = netContribution / revenue ├Ś 100; aov / cac / cpl per channel for the ┬¦14.1 funnel ("Costo de adquisici├│n ŌåÆ conversi├│n ŌåÆ AOV ŌåÆ margen bruto ŌåÆ comisi├│n ŌåÆ costo operativo ŌåÆ margen neto").
- The Meta Business Agent decision (study ┬¦13.1) is documented, configurable, and auditable: `META_AGENT_STRATEGY=own_stack` is the default with a 4-point rationale; switching to `hybrid` or `meta_native` is a deploy-time decision via `.env`; `shouldEscalateToOwnAgent()` provides the hybrid-mode routing predicate.
- Out of scope (next sprint): wire the `shouldEscalateToOwnAgent()` predicate into the orchestrator's intent-classification step so hybrid mode actually routes; replace the placeholder cost estimates in `recordDailyChannelCosts` with real data from Meta API (message fees), LLM usage logs (aiTokenCost), ad-platform adapters (adSpend), agent-time tracking (supportCost); add a `Lead` table so `cpl` isn't a proxy on `ordersCount`; expose `/api/finance/channel-contribution` in the overview dashboard view (frontend ŌĆö out of scope per rules).
